chapter: Chapter 5: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Indian textile industry before British rule.
questions.answer: The Indian textile industry was famous for producing high-quality cotton and silk fabrics that were exported to many countries.

questions.question: Describe the work of Indian weavers and their role in the economy.
questions.answer: Indian weavers used handlooms to produce cotton and silk cloth and played an important role in local and international trade.

questions.question: Explain why Indian textiles were popular in international markets.
questions.answer: Indian textiles were valued for their fine quality, attractive designs and durability.

questions.question: Explain the weaving process used by traditional weavers.
questions.answer: The weaving process involved interlacing threads on a handloom to produce cloth.

questions.question: Explain the problems faced by Indian weavers during British rule.
questions.answer: Indian weavers faced competition from cheaper machine-made cloth and gradually lost their markets.

questions.question: Explain how British industrialization affected Indian weavers.
questions.answer: British industrialization introduced machine-made textiles which reduced the demand for handloom products.

questions.question: Describe the work of iron smelters in traditional industries.
questions.answer: Iron smelters produced iron from iron ore using furnaces and charcoal obtained from forests.

questions.question: Explain why forests were important for iron smelters.
questions.answer: Forests provided wood which was used to produce charcoal required for iron smelting.

questions.question: Explain why the iron smelting industry declined during colonial rule.
questions.answer: The industry declined because of forest restrictions and the availability of cheaper imported iron.

questions.question: Explain the role of factory owners in industrial development.
questions.answer: Factory owners invested money in machines and labour to produce goods in large quantities.

questions.question: Explain the growth of factory industries in India.
questions.answer: Factories using machines were established in cities to produce goods on a large scale.

questions.question: Explain the difference between handloom and machine production.
questions.answer: Handloom production involves manual weaving while machine production uses machines to produce cloth quickly.

questions.question: Explain how machines changed the textile industry.
questions.answer: Machines increased production speed and reduced costs but affected traditional weavers.

questions.question: Explain the impact of industrialization on traditional workers.
questions.answer: Industrialization reduced the demand for traditional crafts and forced many workers to change occupations.

questions.question: Explain how Indian weavers tried to survive during the decline of weaving.
questions.answer: Some weavers adapted by producing different types of cloth or working in textile mills.

questions.question: Explain the importance of textile mills in India.
questions.answer: Textile mills produced cloth using machines and helped develop modern industries in India.

questions.question: Explain how technological changes influenced industries.
questions.answer: Technological changes improved efficiency and increased production in industries.

questions.question: Explain how factory industries affected traditional production systems.
questions.answer: Factory industries introduced machine production which gradually replaced traditional hand-based industries.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between industrialization and economic change.
questions.answer: Industrialization changed economic activities by introducing machines and large-scale production.

questions.question: Explain how British industrialization influenced Indian industries.
questions.answer: British industrialization introduced machine-made goods which competed with Indian products.

questions.question: Explain the role of traditional industries in local economies.
questions.answer: Traditional industries provided employment and produced goods used by local communities.

questions.question: Explain the importance of machines in factory industries.
questions.answer: Machines increased speed, efficiency and production capacity in factories.

questions.question: Explain how industrialization brought changes in employment patterns.
questions.answer: Industrialization created factory jobs but reduced employment in traditional crafts.

questions.question: Explain how factory industries transformed production systems.
questions.answer: Factories introduced organized production using machines and labour working together.

questions.question: Explain the overall impact of industrialization on Indian industries.
questions.answer: Industrialization transformed production by introducing machines, factories and large-scale manufacturing.
